The Hub

Adaptive Equipment Resource Center

United Spinal Association of Tennessee

Free refurbished wheelchairs, mobility devices, and adaptive equipment for people with spinal cord injuries and related disabilities.

across Tennessee

The Problem

The Equipment Gap Is a Crisis

Millions of Americans with spinal cord injuries and mobility disabilities need specialized equipment to live independently. But rising costs, insurance barriers, and long wait times leave too many people without the wheelchairs, walkers, and adaptive devices they need.

The Hub exists to close that gap. We collect, refurbish, and redistribute equipment to Tennesseans who need it — completely free of charge.

Learn more about our mission
$15,000–$70,000
Average cost of a power wheelchair
Out of reach for most families without insurance coverage
6–12 Months
Typical insurance approval wait
Leaving people without essential mobility equipment
40%
Medicare claims denied
Complex documentation requirements exclude eligible people
300,000+
Tennesseans with mobility disabilities
Many unable to access equipment through traditional channels

How Can We Help?

Whether you need equipment, want to give back, or are looking for a loved one — we're here.

Need Equipment?

Our closet provides refurbished mobility equipment at no cost to eligible Tennesseans.

Want to Donate?

Your donated equipment can help someone regain independence and mobility.

Browse Inventory

See what's currently available in our closet. Create an account for full access.

How It Works

Three simple steps to getting the equipment you need.

Step 1

Tell Us What You Need

Submit a request through our simple online form. Let us know what equipment would help improve your daily life.

Step 2

We Match & Prepare

Our team reviews available inventory, inspects equipment quality, and prepares the right match for you.

Step 3

You Receive Equipment

Pick up from our Nashville location or arrange delivery. Always free — no cost, no strings attached.

View Full Process Details

Stories From Our Community

Real impact. Real people. Hear from those whose lives The Hub has touched.

“

The Hub provided me with a power wheelchair within weeks of my discharge from the hospital. I don't know how I would have managed without their help. This equipment gave me my independence back.

Marcus T.
Equipment Recipient, Nashville
“

As an occupational therapist, I refer my SCI patients to The Hub regularly. The team is incredibly responsive and the equipment quality is excellent. It's made a real difference for patients who can't afford retail prices.

Jennifer R., OTR/L
Occupational Therapist, Vanderbilt
“

Donating my late husband's wheelchair felt meaningful because I knew it would help someone in Tennessee. The staff handled everything professionally and with great care.

Sandra K.
Equipment Donor, Memphis

* Stories are representative of community experiences. Names changed for privacy.

Trusted. Funded. Free.

The Hub operates with full nonprofit accountability and national backing.

Funded by
Craig H. Neilsen Foundation

Proudly supported by a national leader in spinal cord injury research and programs.

Affiliated with
United Spinal Association

Tennessee chapter of the nation's leading organization serving people with SCI.

Always
Free to Recipients

Equipment, supplies, and services provided at absolutely no cost to qualified individuals.
